key_insight: Detonation-sprayed Fe-based amorphous coatings with disk-like, highly spread droplets form denser microstructures that improve corrosion and wear performance, while porosity undermines long-term protection.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing spray parameters and ambient humidity can directly tune coating durability for carbon-steel protection in corrosive and tribological service.
method: Detonation-sprayed Fe-based amorphous coatings onto Q235 carbon steel under different spray parameters
Compared coating microstructure, porosity, and droplet morphology
Tested long-term corrosion and tribology behavior under different humidity conditions
evidence_signal: Abstract-level results indicate that higher droplet circularity/expansion produces finer lamellar, denser coatings, and that friction coefficient and wear rate decrease as humidity increases.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on porosity control and environmental humidity, so results may not transfer directly to all service conditions or spray setups.
paper_title: Long-term corrosion behaviors and wear behavior of Fe-based amorphous coatings in different humidity environments
